generated_oml:
// C1: Physical Components
instance StyrofoamBox : DTDFVocab:SystemComponent [
    base:desc "The insulated styrofoam container with a volume of 0.03 m³ for energy retention."
]

instance HeatBed : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Distributed heat source (214 mm × 214 mm × 3 mm) capable of heating air from 293 K (20°C) to 300 K (26.85°C) by producing ≥200 J of energy (100 W power supply)."
]

instance Fan : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Fan circulating air at 0.02 m³/s for uniform temperature distribution."
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B (GPIO-controlled via optocouplers) as the controller for actuating the heatbed, fan, and reading sensors."
]

instance TemperatureSensorInternal : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 temperature sensor measuring air temperature inside the styrofoam box (range: -55°C to +125°C, accuracy ±0.5°C between -10°C and +85°C)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataInternal
]

instance TemperatureSensorHottestSpot : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 temperature sensor positioned at the hottest spot (rearranged for uniformity testing)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataHottestSpot
]

instance TemperatureSensorExternal : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 temperature sensor measuring room temperature outside the styrofoam box."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataExternal
]

instance PowerMeasurement : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Voltage and current measurements (V, I) for energy balance calculations."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PowerData
]

instance FanVoltageMeasurement : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Fan voltage measurements (12V/18V) for airflow regulation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData FanVoltageData
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatBedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "GPIO-controlled MOSFETs via optocouplers to modulate 100 W power delivery to the heatbed."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "GPIO-controlled fan regulation via 12V/18V voltage settings for uniform temperature distribution."
]

// C3: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureDataInternal : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensorInternal
]

instance TemperatureDataHottestSpot : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensorHottestSpot
]

instance TemperatureDataExternal : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensorExternal
]

instance PowerData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PowerMeasurement
]

instance FanVoltageData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FanVoltageMeasurement
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated temperature measurements (T₃, T₂, T₁) from all sensors for real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataInternal, TemperatureDataHottestSpot, TemperatureDataExternal
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler
]

instance PowerDataWithEvents : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Power/current measurements synchronized with heating/fan status events."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PowerData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler
]

instance FanVoltageDataWithEvents : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Fan voltage measurements synchronized with actuator status events."
    DTDFVocab:fromData FanVoltageData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler
]

// C4: Enablers
instance RabbitMQEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time message broker for synchronizing data flows between Physical Twin and Digital Twin via GPIO-controlled components."
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureDataFlowService, PowerDataFlowService, FanVoltageFlowService
]

instance PythonScipyEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Non-linear least squares optimization (Python Scipy) for model calibration using experimental data."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService
]

instance LowLevelDriversEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low-level drivers for sensor/actuator communication (GPIO, 1-Wire bus, optocouplers)."
    DTDFVocab:enables SensorActuatorCommunicationService
]

// C5: Services
instance TemperatureRegulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring and optimization of temperature via bang-bang controller (adjusting H/C based on sensor inputs)."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureControlInsight, TemperatureUniformityAction
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Non-linear least squares optimization of Model A (2 parameters) and Model B (4 parameters) using experimental temperature trajectories."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelParameterOptimizationInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Support for plotting temperature trends (e.g., Fig. 10, 12) and model validation via RabbitMQ synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureTrendVisualizationAction
]

instance SensorActuatorCommunicationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time synchronization of sensor readings and actuator status (heating/fan) for DT monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides EventSynchronizationInsight
]

// C6: Models/Data
instance ModelAThermal : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified thermal model (2 parameters: G_box, C_air) assuming all energy transferred to air."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonScipyEnabler
]

instance ModelBThermal : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Detailed thermal model (4 parameters: G_box, G_heater, C_air, C_heater) accounting for heatbed thermal inertia."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonScipyEnabler
]

instance TemperatureTrajectoriesData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Experimental temperature trajectories (T₃, T₂, T₁) from Figs. 10 and 12 for model validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonScipyEnabler
]

instance PowerBalanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Voltage/current measurements for energy balance calculations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PowerDataWithEvents
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonScipyEnabler
]

// C7: Additional Data instances for the missing services
instance TemperatureDataFlowService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service handling temperature data flow from RabbitMQ to DT."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureData
]

instance PowerDataFlowService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service handling power data flow from RabbitMQ to DT."
    DTDFVocab:provides PowerDataWithEvents
]

instance FanVoltageFlowService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service handling fan voltage data flow from RabbitMQ to DT."
    DTDFVocab:provides FanVoltageDataWithEvents
]

// C8: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time adjustments to heating duration (H) and waiting duration (C) based on temperature thresholds (LL, UL)."
]

instance TemperatureUniformityAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjust fan voltage (12V/18V) to optimize airflow uniformity based on hottest spot sensor readings."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ModelParameterOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Non-linear least squares optimization results for G_box, C_air, G_heater, C_heater to minimize residuals between simulated and experimental temperature trajectories."
]

instance EventSynchronizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Synchronized heating/fan status events with sensor readings for real-time DT monitoring."
]

instance TemperatureTrendVisualizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Plotting temperature trends (T₃, T₂, T₁) and model validation plots via RabbitMQ data synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C9: Environments
instance PhysicalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains StyrofoamBox, HeatBed, Fan, RaspberryPiController
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RabbitMQEnabler, PythonScipyEnabler, LowLevelDriversEnabler,
        TemperatureRegulationService, ModelCalibrationService, VisualizationService,
        ModelAThermal, ModelBThermal, TemperatureDataFlowService, PowerDataFlowService, FanVoltageFlowService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT interacts with the PT via:
  - **Control commands** (heatbed/fan actuation) sent via **RabbitMQ** to the Raspberry Pi’s low-level driver.
  - **Bang-bang control logic**: DT generates **H (heating duration)** and **C (waiting duration)** commands based on:
    - **Lower Limit (LL)**: Minimum acceptable temperature.
    - **Upper Limit (UL)**: Maximum acceptable temperature.
  - **Optocouplers** ensure safe isolation of control signals from high-current MOSFETs."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on:
  - **Real-time synchronization**: Data sampling and actuation occur at **~2-second intervals** (fan airflow completes air circulation in the box).
  - **Model update frequency**: Calibration via **non-linear least squares** (optimization iterations) occurs post-experiment (offline) to adjust parameters for improved accuracy."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is **decentralized**:
  - **Physical components**: PT (Raspberry Pi + sensors/actuators) and **RabbitMQ server** (co-located on the Pi).
  - **Virtual components**: DT components (calibration algorithms, visualization) run independently but communicate via RabbitMQ.
  - **Orchestration**: DT services (e.g., model updates) are triggered by PT data streams."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans:
  - **Design phase**: Model development (Assumptions 1–4) and calibration (Model A/B).
  - **Operational phase**: Real-time monitoring/control via the **bang-bang controller** and RabbitMQ communication."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured via:
  - **Model validation**:
    - **Assumption 1 (uniform temperature)**: Confirmed via experiments (Figs. 10, 12) with max discrepancy **≤7°C** (12V fan) or **6°C** (18V fan).
    - **Model calibration**: Non-linear least squares minimizes residual error (cost function *J*) to match experimental trajectories.
  - **Uncertainty**: Assumptions 3–4 (e.g., constant *c_air*, negligible fan heat) reduce model complexity but limit precision."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT connects to the PT via:
  - **Network protocol**: **RabbitMQ** (message broker) for asynchronous communication (PT → DT: sensor data; DT → PT: control commands).
  - **Physical interface**: **GPIO pins** (Raspberry Pi) for sensor/actuator I/O.
  - **Data flow**: Temperature readings (T₃, T₂, T₁) + power metrics (V, I) streamed at **~2-second intervals** for real-time DT updates."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is deployed on:
  - **Single-node architecture**: Raspberry Pi 4 Model B hosts:
    - **RabbitMQ server** (message broker).
    - **Low-level driver** (sensor/actuator abstraction).
    - **Controller** (bang-bang logic).
  - **Open-source availability**: Full hardware/software specs accessible via [GitHub](https://github.com/INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Standardization involves:
  - **Protocols**:
    - RabbitMQ (open-source message broker),
    - 1-Wire bus (DS18S20 sensors),
    - GPIO (Raspberry Pi interfacing).
  - **Tools**: No formal engineering standards (e.g., ISO/IEEE) are referenced; tools are standard open-source components."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed via:
  - **Hardware Safety**:
    - Optocouplers decouple Raspberry Pi GPIO signals from high-power MOSFETs (4A isolation).
    - Low-level driver incorporates a **safe protocol** to prevent unsafe states.
  - **Operational Safety**:
    - RabbitMQ runs on-premises (Raspberry Pi) with no explicit remote execution capability.
    - Human intervention is implied for critical failures (e.g., manual adjustments if DT detects violations)."
]
